title: 36" Corner Base Kitchen Cabinet (Easier Build)
summary: A simplified square-cornered base cabinet for a kitchen's inside corner, built from a single sheet of plywood with careful layout.
description:
This is the "easier" version of a corner base cabinet — square, not a lazy-Susan or diagonal-front design — and the whole plan hinges on getting every part out of one sheet of plywood, so cut order matters. Cut the bottom off first to establish your two 34-1/2" side pieces, then work the remaining sheet for the top and bottom; plan your cuts on paper before you touch a saw; there's very little margin for a mis-cut piece.

The top and bottom both need a notch cut into their back corner for the back support boards, and that notch has to be cut with a jigsaw after the main rectangular cuts — trying to cut it any other way on an inside corner is how you either blow past your line or leave the corner rounded when it needs to be square. Leave the 5" gap under the bottom shelf exactly as specified; that gap is what lets this cabinet's toe-kick and support structure clear the corner properly.

tools.note: Cuts the toekick openings and the back-corner notches — this cabinet can't be built with straight cuts alone.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", finished, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Every carcass part comes from this one sheet — plan your cut layout before you start.

materials.name: Poplar or hardwood, 1x2, 10 ft
materials.note: Face frame — matched to your door species.

cutList.note: Back corner notched with a jigsaw to fit the back supports.

steps.title: Plan and cut the carcass from one sheet
steps.body: Every piece in this cabinet comes from a single sheet of plywood, so plan your cut layout on paper first — there's very little margin for a wasted cut. Cut the bottom off the sheet first to establish the two 34-1/2" side pieces, then cut the remaining sheet for the top and bottom (34-3/4" square each). You'll need a jigsaw to cut the toekick opening and the inside-corner notch on the top and bottom — see the next step for exact placement.

steps.title: Notch the back corner
steps.body: The back corner of both the top and bottom needs a notch cut to fit the back support boards. Leftover scrap plywood from ripping the sides works for these supports (about 3-1/2" wide), or use 1x4 boards. Notch the corner out with a jigsaw to fit whichever you choose.

steps.title: Assemble the carcass
steps.body: Before assembly, drill 3/4" pocket holes on the outsides of the sides, top, and bottom — you'll need these for the face frame later. Attach the top and bottom to the sides, leaving the specified 5" gap underneath for the bottom shelf support — that gap is what lets the toe-kick and support structure clear the corner.

steps.title: Attach the back supports
steps.body: Attach the back support boards to the top and bottom, maintaining the same 5" gap under the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Add the back panels
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood back panels with 3/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame separately, then attach it to the front of the cabinet — it overextends the sides of the cabinet by 1/4", matching the reveal on the rest of the kitchen run.

steps.title: Hang the door
steps.body: Install a full-overlay door sized to the opening, using concealed overlay hinges.

images.alt: Square corner base kitchen cabinet with a face frame and a single overlay door
